summary_draft: U.S.-based bitcoin exchange that faced recurring banking and regulatory problems and later ceased operations; the current terminal handling should stay conservative and evidence-led.

non_archive.title: Bank Stops Working With Bitcoin Exchange CampBX Due to 'Regulatory Uncertainty'
Department of Banking and Finance Orders CampBX, Bitcoin Trading Platform, to Cease and Desist
non_archive.publisher: Georgia Department of Banking and Finance

non_archive.title: Atlanta-Based Crypto Exchange Still Owes Clients 4 Years After Closing
non_archive.purpose: later reporting on shutdown aftermath and unresolved customer funds

evidence_notes: Keep closure timing conservative until a cleaner official end-state pin is secured.
Regulation is the current best primary-cause label because the 2018 Georgia action is strong primary evidence.
Archive plus at least two non-archive sources remain required before public_ready promotion.

event_candidates.title: Georgia cease and desist became final
event_candidates.description: The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance said its cease and desist order against CampBX became final on 2018-07-25.

event_candidates.title: ACH and wire transfers halted
event_candidates.description: CampBX halted ACH and wire transfers after its bank decided not to work with bitcoin businesses because of regulatory uncertainty.

risks: exact launch date still not pinned
exact terminal operations date may differ from regulatory final-order date
aftermath reporting is weaker than the official 2018 enforcement document
final_notes: Need a stronger archived official site capture around the final shutdown period.
If stronger evidence surfaces, revisit whether death_date should track earlier operational cessation rather than the 2018 final-order date.
